title: Agropyron × saxicola
intro: Agropyron × saxicola, also known as sand dropseed, is a hybrid grass species native to the Great Plains region of North America. The health benefits of Agropyron × saxicola include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Agropyron × saxicola include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
taxonomy: Agropyron × saxicola is classified as a species of grass within the genus Agropyron and the family Poaceae, commonly known as wheat grasses. It is a hybrid of Agropyron cristatum and Agropyron junceum. This species is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, and it is known for its ability to grow in areas with poor soil quality. Agropyron × saxicola is a perennial grass that can grow up to 1 meter in height and has a spreading habit. It is often used as a ornamental plant in landscaping due to its attractive flowers and ability to thrive in challenging conditions.

preparations: Agropyron × saxicola, also known as needle-and-thread grass, has been used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures.
